name: Suspicious Scheduled Task
notes:
This rule detects suspicious scheduled task creation or update events based on attributes such as paths, commands line flags, etc.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious scheduled task creation events. Based on attributes such as paths, commands line flags, etc.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Update
SIGMA Description: Detects update to a scheduled task event that contain suspicious keywords.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5000876) Success Audit: A scheduled task was updated, (5000872) Success Audit: A scheduled task was created

when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [regsvr32 or rundll32 or cmd or powershell or pwsh or mshta or wscript or cscript or certutil or bitsadmin or bash or scrcons or wmic or forfiles or scriptrunner or hh]

when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%appdata%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\users\public%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%temp%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\desktop%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\downloads%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\programdata%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\perflogs%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Scheduled Task on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5000876) Success Audit: A scheduled task was updated, (5000872) Success Audit: A scheduled task was created

AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [regsvr32 or rundll32 or cmd or powershell or pwsh or mshta or wscript or cscript or certutil or bitsadmin or bash or scrcons or wmic or forfiles or scriptrunner or hh]

AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%appdata%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\users\public%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%temp%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\desktop%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\downloads%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\programdata%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%\perflogs%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: This rule detects suspicious scheduled task creation or update events based on attributes such as paths, commands line flags, etc.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
